activities_projects.title: Orbital Geometry & Bond Angles
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the spatial arrangement of carbon bonds in different allotropes
steps: Build a tetrahedral model of Methane ($CH_4$) and measure the bond angles
Compare this to the planar hexagonal structure of Graphite
Research why Diamond has a bond angle of $109.5^\circ$ while Graphite's is $120^\circ$
Explain the relationship between bond angle, hybridization (sp3 vs sp2), and physical hardness
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates how molecular geometry dictates the extreme differences in physical properties of carbon allotropes
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical explanation of bond angles and their impact on structural rigidity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: IUPAC Nomenclature of Complex Isomers
activities_projects.objective: Master naming rules for branched and functionalized hydrocarbons
steps: Draw all structural isomers for Hexane ($C_6H_{14}$)
Apply IUPAC rules to name each (e.g., 2-methylpentane, 2,3-dimethylbutane)
Identify the 'longest carbon chain' and the lowest locant rule for substituents
Draw a molecule containing both a ketone group and a methyl branch and name it correctly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates precision in identifying and naming structural isomers of higher alkanes
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the 5 structural isomers of hexane and their corresponding IUPAC names (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mechanism of Addition Reactions
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the industrial hydrogenation of vegetable oils
materials: Chemical equation of Ethene to Ethane conversion
steps: Study the reaction: $CH_2=CH_2 + H_2 \xrightarrow{Ni/Pd} CH_3-CH_3$
Explain the role of the catalyst in breaking the $\pi$-bond of the alkene
Discuss the health implications of 'trans-fats' produced during partial hydrogenation of oils
Calculate the mass of Hydrogen required to saturate 1 mole of Ethene
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the chemical process of converting liquid unsaturated fats into solid saturated fats
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed description of the catalytic mechanism and health-related research findings (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Esterification Equilibrium
activities_projects.objective: Understand the reversible nature of ester formation and yield optimization
steps: Perform the esterification reaction and isolate the ester using a separating funnel
Calculate the theoretical yield of Ethyl Acetate from 5g of Ethanol
Research why concentrated $H_2SO_4$ acts as both a catalyst and a dehydrating agent
Apply Le Chatelier's Principle to explain how removing water increases ester yield
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes knowledge of chemical equilibrium and stoichiometry in organic synthesis
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct calculation of theoretical yield and explanation of the dehydrating agent's role (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Saponification: Quantitative Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Determine the saponification value of different oils
steps: Boil a known mass of oil with an excess of alcoholic $KOH$
Titrate the unreacted $KOH$ against standard $HCl$
Calculate the amount of $KOH$ consumed by the oil
Relate the saponification value to the average chain length of fatty acids in the oil
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies quantitative titration to determine the chemical characteristics of fats
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of titration data and calculation of the saponification value (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Combustion Analysis of Hydrocarbons
activities_projects.objective: Determine the empirical formula of an unknown fuel
materials: Data set: Mass of $CO_2$ and $H_2O$ produced by burning 1g of fuel
steps: Use the mass of $CO_2$ to find the mass of Carbon in the sample
Use the mass of $H_2O$ to find the mass of Hydrogen
Calculate the mole ratio of C to H
Determine if the fuel is an alkane, alkene, or alkyne based on the ratio
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses quantitative combustion data to identify the class of organic compounds
activities_projects.evaluation: Mathematical derivation of the empirical formula from the provided data (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Properties of Denatured Spirit
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the chemical additives used to prevent ethanol misuse
steps: Research why Methanol is highly toxic to the human optic nerve
Explain why Copper Sulphate is added to industrial ethanol (visual warning)
Identify the chemical formula of Pyridine and why it is added to 'denature' the spirit
Draft a warning label explaining the chemical dangers of consuming industrial alcohol
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the social and biological implications of industrial chemical additives
activities_projects.evaluation: Comprehensive report on the toxicity of methanol and the purpose of denaturing (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Structure-Activity Relationship of Micelles
steps: Slowly add soap to distilled water while passing a laser beam through it
Identify the exact concentration where the Tyndall effect becomes visible (indicating micelle formation)
Research how temperature (Krafft point) affects this concentration
Explain the thermodynamics of why the hydrophobic tails cluster together
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the physical chemistry behind the aggregation of surfactant molecules
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the Tyndall effect in relation to CMC and entropy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Oxidizing Agents: Chromic Acid vs. Permanganate
activities_projects.objective: Compare the efficiency of different agents in producing carboxylic acids
steps: Perform the oxidation of propanol with both agents in separate setups
Observe the color change from orange to green ($Cr^{6+} ightarrow Cr^{3+}$)
Test the final product with $NaHCO_3$ to confirm acid formation
Research which agent is more selective for producing aldehydes vs. acids
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between the strengths and color indicators of major oxidizing agents
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of oxidation states and product testing results (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Polymerization Research Project
activities_projects.objective: Study the formation of Polythene and PVC from monomers
steps: Draw the structure of the Ethene monomer and its repeating unit in Polythene
Contrast this with Vinyl Chloride and its polymer PVC
Research the difference between Addition Polymerization and Condensation Polymerization
Categorize common household plastics based on their recycling codes and chemical structure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the link between small organic monomers and large industrial polymers
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed structural diagrams of monomers and their corresponding polymers (10 marks)
